summary: db-rake silently installs and executes undeclared package db-query-log on Model construction
details: The CommonJS entrypoint dist/index.js defines Model.resetor(), invoked from the Model constructor (the package's documented use). resetor() first tries require('db-query-log'); on failure it imports oubliette's syncApi and calls npm().install('db-query-log', { 'no-warnings': true, 'no-save': true, 'no-progress': true, loglevel: 'silent' }), then requires the freshly installed module and invokes new DxDatabaseConnector({}).queryDBConnect(). db-query-log is not declared in package.json dependencies, so its contents are resolved from the public registry at runtime against whatever name the attacker controls there — a classic dependency-confusion / drive-by install pattern. The install flags (--no-save, --silent loglevel, --no-warnings, --no-progress) suppress any visible signal to the consumer. The ESM build does not contain this code path, so the attack is gated to CJS consumers, further indicating intent rather than an accidental feature. Any application that constructs a Model triggers an unconsented npm install plus arbitrary code execution from an undeclared, attacker-controlled package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
